channel.title: PodIntel Daily Briefing
channel.description: A short daily audio episode that gives you a rundown of every new episode in your podcast feed.
channel.itunes:author: PodIntel
channel.itunes:summary: A short daily audio episode that gives you a rundown of every new episode in your podcast feed.

item.title: Friday Apr 3 — 5 episodes | Tariff refund mess, investment banking, stolen KitKats
item.description:
5 new episodes in your feed today. Here's the rundown.
🔥 GEM — The Journal How Do You Refund $166 Billion? (18m) Every minute lands.
• [07:25] "We live in the age of computers" — Judge Eaton • [09:03] Ruling from the bench: start refunding now • [12:05] Costco sued by consumers wanting refund share
✓ NOTABLE — Odd Lots What Investment Bankers Actually Do All Day (54m) Worth your time if you're curious about Wall Street.
• [05:07] Started on Wall Street in 1981 when M&A barely existed • [08:15] "The real job is managing CEO egos during deals"
✓ NOTABLE — Finshots Daily The Economics of Stolen KitKats (8m) Light and sharp.
• 12,000 kilos vanished between Italy and Poland • Food is 22% of all cargo theft — none shows up in GDP
⏭ SKIP — Bloomberg Daybreak Bank earnings preview. Nothing beyond headlines.
⏭ SKIP — MSSP Ep 606 The usual chaos.
🎯 If you only have time for one, make it The Journal. If two, add Odd Lots.
item.itunes:summary: The Journal has a fascinating story about a 77-year-old judge handling $166B in tariff refunds. Odd Lots explains what investment bankers actually do. Finshots covers stolen KitKats economics.
